title: Deep learning versus health-care professionals in detecting disease from medical imaging: a diagnostic accuracy meta-analysis
citation: Modeled on Liu X et al., 'A comparison of deep learning performance against health-care professionals in detecting diseases from medical imaging', Lancet Digital Health 2019;1(6):e271-e297. Per-study discrimination and accuracy values are representative test data structured to match the review's design (AUROC, sensitivity, specificity reported per study; not pooled with inverse-variance methods); they are NOT verbatim transcriptions. Grounding is evaluated against the source text in this fixture.

protocol.researchQuestion: For detection of disease from medical imaging, how does the diagnostic performance of deep learning models compare with health-care professionals?

template.name: Diagnostic model performance
template.instructions: Extract the model's reported discrimination (AUROC, with 95% CI when available) and diagnostic accuracy (sensitivity and specificity) for each study.

outcomes.name: Discrimination
outcomes.description: Area under the ROC curve for the deep learning model.

outcomes.name: Diagnostic accuracy
outcomes.description: Sensitivity and specificity of the deep learning model at the reported operating point.

studies.label: DL diabetic retinopathy
studies.source: Deep learning for detection of diabetic retinopathy from fundus photographs. On the external validation set, the model achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.93 (95% CI, 0.90 to 0.95). At the operating threshold, sensitivity was 0.91 and specificity was 0.88.

expected.quote: area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.93 (95% CI, 0.90 to 0.95)

studies.label: DL skin cancer
studies.source: Deep learning classification of skin lesions. The convolutional neural network reached an AUROC of 0.95 (95% CI, 0.93 to 0.97) on the held-out test set, with sensitivity of 0.94 and specificity of 0.90.

studies.label: DL chest radiograph
studies.source: Deep learning detection of pneumonia on chest radiographs. The model's discrimination was an AUROC of 0.88 (95% CI, 0.84 to 0.92). Reported sensitivity was 0.86 and specificity was 0.85.

studies.label: DL mammography
studies.source: Deep learning for breast cancer detection on mammography. The system achieved an AUROC of 0.91 (95% CI, 0.88 to 0.94), with a sensitivity of 0.89 and a specificity of 0.87 at the chosen operating point.

studies.label: DL CT nodule
studies.source: Deep learning for pulmonary nodule malignancy classification on CT. On external validation the model obtained an AUROC of 0.90 (95% CI, 0.86 to 0.94); sensitivity was 0.88 and specificity was 0.84.
